prayer.es:
San Juan Pablo II, pastor de la Iglesia universal y apÃ³stol de la misericordia, que recorriste el mundo entero repitiendo sin cesar: Â«Â¡No tengÃ¡is miedo, abrid las puertas a Cristo!Â»

TÃº que perdonaste de corazÃ³n a quien atentÃ³ contra tu vida, ensÃ©Ã±ame el poder del perdÃ³n verdadero. TÃº que consagraste tu vida entera a MarÃ­a con las palabras Totus Tuus â€” Â«Todo tuyoÂ» â€” llÃ©vame tambiÃ©n tÃº a los pies de la Madre.

prayer.en:
Saint John Paul II, shepherd of the universal Church and apostle of mercy, who traveled the entire world repeating without ceasing: 'Do not be afraid! Open wide the doors to Christ!'

You who knew suffering from an early age â€” the loss of your family, war, persecution â€” and never ceased to trust in God or to love humanity, intercede for me today.

You who forgave from the heart the one who attempted your life, teach me the power of true forgiveness. You who consecrated your entire life to Mary with the words Totus Tuus â€” 'All yours' â€” lead me also to the feet of the Mother.

Saint John Paul II, intercede for me, for my family, for the Church, and for the world. May I learn to have no fear and to open wide the door to Christ every day of my life.

Amen.

history.es:
Karol Jozef Wojtyla nacio el 18 de mayo de 1920 en Wadowice, Polonia, el menor de tres hijos de un oficial del ejercito polaco y una maestra. Su infancia estuvo marcada por el dolor: su madre murio cuando el tenia 9 anos, su hermano mayor Edmund fallecio de escarlatina cuando Karol tenia 12, y su padre —su gran modelo espiritual— murio en 1941 cuando el joven tenia 21. Durante la ocupacion nazi de Polonia, Wojtyla trabajo en una cantera de piedra y luego en una fabrica quimica para escapar de la deportacion. En ese periodo de oscuridad comenzo a gestarse su vocacion sacerdotal.

Ordenado sacerdote en 1946, doctor en teologia por la Angelicum de Roma, profesor de etica en la Universidad Catolica de Lublin, Obispo auxiliar de Cracovia en 1958, Arzobispo en 1964 y Cardenal en 1967. Participo activamente en el Concilio Vaticano II y fue figura clave en la redaccion de la constitucion Gaudium et Spes. El 16 de octubre de 1978, a los 58 anos, fue elegido Papa —el primer Papa no italiano en 455 anos y el primer eslavo en la historia—. Eligio el nombre de Juan Pablo II en honor a sus dos predecesores inmediatos.

Su pontificado de 26 anos (1978-2005) fue el tercero mas largo de la historia. Visito 129 paises, fue el Papa mas viajado de la historia. Beatificó y canonizo a mas personas que todos sus predecesores juntos. Contribuyo decisivamente a la caida del comunismo en Europa del Este a traves de su apoyo al sindicato Solidarnosc en Polonia. Sobrevivio al atentado del 13 de mayo de 1981 en la Plaza de San Pedro, perpetrado por el turco Ali Agca, y atribuyo su salvacion a la Virgen de Fatima. Anos despues perdono personalmente a su agresor en la celda de la carcel.

Juan Pablo II sufrio el Parkinson desde 1993 con una dignidad que fue en si misma un testimonio universal sobre el sentido del sufrimiento humano. Murio el 2 de abril de 2005 a las 21:37, vispera de la fiesta de la Divina Misericordia —la misma devocion que el habia establecido para la Iglesia universal—. En su entierro, la multitud grito espontaneamente: «¡Santo subito!». Fue beatificado por Benedicto XVI el 1 de mayo de 2011 y canonizado por el Papa Francisco el 27 de abril de 2014, junto con Juan XXIII.

1. EL ATENTADO Y EL PERDON (13 de mayo de 1981)
En la Plaza de San Pedro, el turco Ali Agca disparo dos veces a Juan Pablo II desde tres metros de distancia. Las balas atravesaron el abdomen del Papa y rozaron la arteria aorta por milimetros. Los medicos dijeron que su supervivencia fue clinicamente inexplicable. Al recuperarse, el Papa consagro su vida entera a la Virgen de Fatima —cuya fiesta era ese mismo 13 de mayo— y visito a Agca en la carcel de Rebibbia. La imagen de los dos hombres tomados de las manos, el pontifice perdonando al asesino, dio la vuelta al mundo. «No te odio. Te perdono», le dijo.

2. LA CAIDA DEL COMUNISMO SOVIETICO (1989)
El papa polaco regreso a Polonia en 1979 ante tres millones de personas y grito en Varsovia: «No tengais miedo». Aquel grito encendio la mecha del sindicato Solidaridad liderado por Lech Walesa. Margaret Thatcher dijo: «Sin el Papa, no hay Solidaridad. Sin Solidaridad, no hay Gorbachov. Sin Gorbachov, el Muro de Berlin sigue en pie». El 9 de noviembre de 1989 el Muro cayo sin que se disparara un solo tiro. Juan Pablo II atribuyo todo a la Virgen de Fatima y a la consagracion del mundo al Inmaculado Corazon.

3. «SANTO SUBITO!» — LA CANONIZACION MAS RAPIDA DEL SIGLO
Cuando Juan Pablo II murio el 2 de abril de 2005, la Plaza de San Pedro se llenó de millones de peregrinos que coreaban: «Santo subito! ¡Santo ahora!». El proceso fue el mas rapido del siglo: Benedicto XVI lo beatificó el 1 de mayo de 2011, y Francisco lo canonizo el 27 de abril de 2014 junto con Juan XXIII. En el momento de su beatificacion, una enfermera francesa llamada Marie Simon-Pierre, enferma del Parkinson, fue curada instantaneamente tras encomendarse a el. El milagro fue reconocido por la Santa Sede.
history.en:
Karol Jozef Wojtyla was born on May 18, 1920, in Wadowice, Poland, the youngest of three children of a Polish army officer and a schoolteacher. His childhood was marked by grief: his mother died when he was 9, his elder brother Edmund died of scarlet fever when Karol was 12, and his father — his great spiritual model — died in 1941 when the young man was 21. During the Nazi occupation of Poland, Wojtyla worked in a stone quarry and later in a chemical factory to escape deportation. It was during this period of darkness that his priestly vocation began to take shape.

Ordained a priest in 1946, he earned a doctorate in theology from the Angelicum in Rome, taught ethics at the Catholic University of Lublin, became Auxiliary Bishop of Krakow in 1958, Archbishop in 1964, and Cardinal in 1967. He actively participated in the Second Vatican Council and was a key figure in drafting the constitution Gaudium et Spes. On October 16, 1978, at the age of 58, he was elected Pope — the first non-Italian Pope in 455 years and the first Slav in history. He chose the name John Paul II in honor of his two immediate predecessors.

His 26-year pontificate (1978-2005) was the third longest in history. He visited 129 countries, making him the most widely-traveled Pope in history. He beatified and canonized more people than all his predecessors combined. He contributed decisively to the fall of communism in Eastern Europe through his support for the Solidarity trade union in Poland. He survived the assassination attempt of May 13, 1981, in St. Peter's Square, carried out by the Turk Ali Agca, and attributed his survival to Our Lady of Fatima. Years later, he personally forgave his attacker in his prison cell.

John Paul II suffered from Parkinson's disease from 1993 with a dignity that was itself a universal testimony to the meaning of human suffering. He died on April 2, 2005, at 9:37 PM, the eve of the feast of Divine Mercy — the very devotion he had established for the universal Church. At his funeral, the crowd spontaneously cried: 'Santo subito!' He was beatified by Benedict XVI on May 1, 2011, and canonized by Pope Francis on April 27, 2014, together with John XXIII.

1. THE ASSASSINATION ATTEMPT AND THE FORGIVENESS (May 13, 1981)
In Saint Peter's Square, Turkish gunman Ali Agca fired twice at John Paul II from three meters away. The bullets pierced the Pope's abdomen and missed the aorta by millimeters. Doctors said his survival was clinically inexplicable. Upon recovering, the Pope consecrated his entire life to Our Lady of Fatima — whose feast fell on that very May 13th — and visited Agca in Rebibbia prison. The image of the two men hand in hand, the pontiff forgiving his would-be killer, circled the globe. "I do not hate you. I forgive you," he told him.

2. THE FALL OF SOVIET COMMUNISM (1989)
The Polish pope returned to Poland in 1979 before three million people and cried out in Warsaw: "Be not afraid." That cry lit the fuse of the Solidarity trade union led by Lech Walesa. Margaret Thatcher said: "Without the Pope, no Solidarity. Without Solidarity, no Gorbachev. Without Gorbachev, the Berlin Wall stands." On November 9, 1989, the Wall fell without a single shot fired. John Paul II attributed everything to Our Lady of Fatima and to the consecration of the world to the Immaculate Heart.

3. "SANTO SUBITO!" — THE FASTEST CANONIZATION OF THE CENTURY
When John Paul II died on April 2, 2005, Saint Peter's Square was filled with millions of pilgrims chanting: "Santo subito! Saint now!" The process was the fastest of the century: Benedict XVI beatified him on May 1, 2011, and Francis canonized him on April 27, 2014, together with John XXIII. At the moment of his beatification, a French nurse named Marie Simon-Pierre, suffering from Parkinson's disease, was instantly cured after commending herself to him. The miracle was recognized by the Holy See.

novena.en:
Day 1 â€” 'Do Not Be Afraid!'

Saint John Paul II, your cry to the world was 'Do not be afraid!' Today I ask you to intercede for me in moments of fear and anguish. May the same courage you received from above fill my heart as well.

Pray: 1 Our Father, 1 Hail Mary, 1 Glory Be.

novena.es:
DÃ­a 2 â€” Totus Tuus

novena.en:
Day 2 â€” Totus Tuus

Saint John Paul II, your life motto was Totus Tuus â€” all yours, Mary. Intercede that I too may entrust myself fully to the Virgin, that she may form me and lead me to her Son.

Pray: 1 Our Father, 1 Hail Mary, 1 Glory Be.

novena.es:
DÃ­a 3 â€” El PerdÃ³n

San Juan Pablo II, que perdonaste de corazÃ³n a quien intentÃ³ quitarte la vida, intercede por mÃ­ para que pueda perdonar a quienes me han herido. Que el perdÃ³n que mÃ¡s cuesta sea el que mÃ¡s libera.

novena.en:
Day 3 â€” Forgiveness

Saint John Paul II, who forgave from the heart the one who tried to take your life, intercede that I may forgive those who have hurt me. May the hardest forgiveness be the one that most sets free.

Pray: 1 Our Father, 1 Hail Mary, 1 Glory Be.

novena.es:
DÃ­a 4 â€” La Divina Misericordia

San Juan Pablo II, gran apÃ³stol de la Divina Misericordia, intercede para que yo me abra completamente a esa misericordia sin lÃ­mites de Dios que es mÃ¡s grande que todos mis errores juntos.

Reza: 1 Padre Nuestro, 1 Ave MarÃ­a, 1 Gloria.
novena.en:
Day 4 â€” Divine Mercy

Saint John Paul II, great apostle of Divine Mercy, intercede that I may open myself fully to God's boundless mercy which is greater than all my mistakes combined.

Pray: 1 Our Father, 1 Hail Mary, 1 Glory Be.

novena.es:
DÃ­a 5 â€” Los JÃ³venes

San Juan Pablo II, que amabas a los jÃ³venes y fundaste las Jornadas Mundiales de la Juventud, intercede por los jÃ³venes que hoy estÃ¡n perdidos o sin esperanza. Que encuentren en Cristo la respuesta a sus preguntas mÃ¡s profundas.

Reza: 1 Padre Nuestro, 1 Ave MarÃ­a, 1 Gloria.
novena.en:
Day 5 â€” Youth

Saint John Paul II, who loved young people and founded World Youth Day, intercede for the young people today who are lost or without hope. May they find in Christ the answer to their deepest questions.

Pray: 1 Our Father, 1 Hail Mary, 1 Glory Be.

novena.es:
DÃ­a 6 â€” El Sufrimiento

San Juan Pablo II, que sufriste en el cuerpo y en el espÃ­ritu durante tus Ãºltimos aÃ±os y jamÃ¡s perdiste la paz, intercede por los enfermos y por quienes lloran una pÃ©rdida. Que el sufrimiento unido a Cristo se convierta en gracia.

novena.en:
Day 6 â€” Suffering

Saint John Paul II, who suffered in body and spirit during your final years yet never lost your peace, intercede for the sick and for those mourning a loss. May suffering united to Christ become grace.

Pray: 1 Our Father, 1 Hail Mary, 1 Glory Be.

novena.es:
DÃ­a 7 â€” La Familia

San Juan Pablo II, que enseÃ±aste que la familia es la Â«Iglesia domÃ©sticaÂ», intercede por mi familia. Que el amor de Cristo reine en mi hogar y sane lo que estÃ¡ roto.

Reza: 1 Padre Nuestro, 1 Ave MarÃ­a, 1 Gloria.
novena.en:
Day 7 â€” The Family

Saint John Paul II, who taught that the family is the 'domestic Church', intercede for my family. May the love of Christ reign in my home and heal what is broken.

Pray: 1 Our Father, 1 Hail Mary, 1 Glory Be.

novena.es:
DÃ­a 8 â€” La Paz en el Mundo

San Juan Pablo II, que trabajaste incansablemente por la paz entre naciones y pueblos, intercede por la paz en el mundo y en los corazones. Que quienes tienen poder elijan el diÃ¡logo sobre la guerra.

Reza: 1 Padre Nuestro, 1 Ave MarÃ­a, 1 Gloria.
novena.en:
Day 8 â€” Peace in the World

Saint John Paul II, who worked tirelessly for peace between nations and peoples, intercede for peace in the world and in hearts. May those who hold power choose dialogue over war.

Pray: 1 Our Father, 1 Hail Mary, 1 Glory Be.

novena.es:

novena.en:
Day 9 â€” Opening the Doors to Christ

Saint John Paul II, on this last day I present to you with all my heart my most urgent intention (pause and present your intention). May I learn from you to have no fear and to open wide the doors of my life to Christ.

Saint John Paul II, pray for us!

Pray: 1 Our Father, 1 Hail Mary, 1 Glory Be.

San Juan Pablo II, heraldo e instituidor de la Fiesta de la Divina Misericordia, ruega por nosotros.
San Juan Pablo II, amante ferviente de la Virgen MarÃ­a con el lema Â«Totus TuusÂ», ruega por nosotros.

Cordero de Dios, que quitas el pecado del mundo, perdÃ³nanos, SeÃ±or.

V. San Juan Pablo II, Pastor de la Iglesia Universal.

Oremos: SeÃ±or, que diste a la Iglesia a San Juan Pablo II como pastor valiente y testigo de la fe: concede que, siguiendo su ejemplo de confianza total en Cristo y de amor a la Virgen MarÃ­a, vivamos entregados a la nueva evangelizaciÃ³n. Por Jesucristo Nuestro SeÃ±or. AmÃ©n.
litany.en:
Litany of Saint John Paul II

Lord, have mercy. Lord, have mercy.
Christ, have mercy. Christ, have mercy.
Lord, have mercy. Lord, have mercy.

God the Father in heaven, have mercy on us.
God the Son, Redeemer of the world, have mercy on us.
God the Holy Spirit, have mercy on us.
Holy Trinity, one God, have mercy on us.

Saint John Paul II, defender of the dignity of every human person, pray for us.
Saint John Paul II, apostle of the new evangelization, pray for us.
Saint John Paul II, who proclaimed Â«Be not afraid!Â», pray for us.
Saint John Paul II, tireless pilgrim of faith on every continent, pray for us.
Saint John Paul II, who bore illness with dignity at the end of your life, pray for us.
Saint John Paul II, herald and institutor of the Feast of Divine Mercy, pray for us.
Saint John Paul II, fervent lover of the Virgin Mary with the motto Â«Totus TuusÂ», pray for us.
Saint John Paul II, who survived an assassination attempt and forgave your attacker, pray for us.
Saint John Paul II, promoter of the dignity of the family and marriage, pray for us.
Saint John Paul II, defender of human rights against dictatorships, pray for us.
Saint John Paul II, apostle of dialogue between religions and cultures, pray for us.
Saint John Paul II, who canonized Saint Faustina and promoted the Theology of the Body, pray for us.
Saint John Paul II, who died on the vigil of the Feast of Divine Mercy, pray for us.

Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, spare us, O Lord.
Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, graciously hear us, O Lord.
Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, have mercy on us.

V. Saint John Paul II, Pastor of the Universal Church.
R. Intercede for the Church and for the whole world.

Let us pray: Lord, who gave the Church Saint John Paul II as a courageous pastor and witness of faith: grant that, following his example of total trust in Christ and love for the Virgin Mary, we may live devoted to the new evangelization. Through Jesus Christ Our Lord. Amen.

consecration.es:

AyÃºdame a no tener miedo, como tÃº proclamaste al mundo entero. AyÃºdame a confiar en la Divina Misericordia, que tÃº tanto promoviste. AyÃºdame a ser testigo de Cristo en el mundo de hoy.

San Juan Pablo II, recibe mi consagraciÃ³n y ponla en manos de la Virgen MarÃ­a, para que ella la lleve ante su Hijo JesÃºs. AmÃ©n.
consecration.en:
Consecration Through the Intercession of Saint John Paul II

O Saint John Paul II, who lived totally consecrated to Christ under the motto Â«Totus TuusÂ» â€” all yours, O Mary â€”: intercede for me so that I too may give myself completely to God.

Help me not to be afraid, as you proclaimed to the whole world. Help me to trust in Divine Mercy, which you so fervently promoted. Help me to be a witness of Christ in the world of today.

Saint John Paul II, receive my consecration and place it in the hands of the Virgin Mary, so that she may carry it before her Son Jesus. Amen.
